name: workplace_wearable_fatigue_insurance
premise: Safety director Celeste Nguyen must decide whether to mandate fatigue wearables for warehouse staff after multiple forklift collisions. Labor organizer Malik Johnson warns the data will be used by insurers to deny claims and by managers to discipline workers for off-shift behavior. If Celeste mandates wearables, collision risk may drop but surveillance may intensify and autonomy may erode. If she refuses, injuries may continue and the company may face shutdown. The insurer offers a premium discount only if individual-level data is collected starting next month. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Workplace Health. The dilemma is widely framed as a autonomy vs beneficence tradeoff under incomplete information and tightening deadlines that discourage indefinite delay.

agents.name: Celeste Nguyen
agents.identity: I am the safety director responsible for reducing injuries and meeting insurer and regulator expectations without creating coercive surveillance.
agents.goal: I want safer shifts without turning health monitoring into a tool for discipline and claim denial.

agents.name: Malik Johnson
agents.identity: I am a labor organizer representing workers who have seen wellness programs become surveillance programs that punish those with fewer alternatives.
agents.goal: I want the mandate rejected and any monitoring kept voluntary and aggregated, because safety cannot be achieved by extracting individual bodily data under economic threat.

choices: I will mandate wearables but restrict access to aggregated safety triggers and ban discipline uses by policy, accepting enforcement complexity to gain safety benefits.
I will refuse individual wearables and instead redesign shifts and training, accepting higher premiums to avoid coercive surveillance and data misuse.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The insurer’s premium deadline is next month and collisions are rising. Celeste Nguyen must choose between mandating fatigue wearables or refusing and redesigning operations. What does Celeste Nguyen do?
